title: citrus medica subf. kizu
intro: Citrus medica subf. kizu, also known as fingered citron, is a rare and unique plant species in the Rutaceae family, characterized by its finger-like extensions of the citron peel. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Citrus medica subf. kizu include its ability to support the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and take advantage of its numerous health benefits.
